{"step_by_step_thinking": "Mogamulizumab is a defucosylated humanized monoclonal antibody that targets the CCR4 chemokine receptor, which is overexpressed on malignant T-cells. Document [0] states that targeted agents approved for mycosis fungoides/Sezary syndrome (which are forms of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma) include mogamulizumab. Document [4] specifically mentions that in adult T-cell leukemia/leukemia (ATL), mogamulizumab is used, and it notes that anti-CCR4 antibody mogamulizumab is used in combination with other therapies for relapsed or refractory cases. While the primary indication in the documents is for ATL, the mechanism of action (targeting CCR4 on malignant T-cells) is directly relevant to cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL), which also involves malignant T-cells expressing CCR4. Document [21] explicitly lists'relapsed or refractory cutaneous T-cell lymphomas' as one of the non-FDA-approved uses of mogamulizumab. This directly supports its use in CTCL. Therefore, based on the evidence, mogamulizumab can be used for the treatment of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma.", "answer_choice": "A"}
